items.title: Match activation to layer role
items.avoid: Defaulting to tanh on every layer including binary outputs and LSTM gates.
items.prefer: Tanh on zero-centered hidden/cell candidates; sigmoid on [0,1] gates and probabilities; ReLU on deep feedforward hidden layers.
items.why: Each activation encodes a range and semantic meaning — mismatches break losses and gate semantics.
items.example: MLP classifier: ReLU hidden + sigmoid output (binary). LSTM: sigmoid gates + tanh candidate cell.

items.title: Keep pre-activations in the linear region early
items.avoid: Letting weights grow unchecked so tanh inputs immediately saturate.
items.prefer: Xavier/Glorot init, learning rate warmup, or normalization so most z start near zero where tanh'(0)=1.
items.why: Gradients are largest near the origin; saturated units learn slowly.
items.example: After init, check |tanh(z)| < 0.5 for most units; if not, reduce init scale or add LayerNorm.

items.title: Use stable tanh implementations
items.avoid: Hand-written exp formulas without overflow guards.
items.why: Numerical stability for extreme inputs and correct autograd.
items.example: Training GAN with custom tanh on large logits → NaN. Switch to torch.tanh fixes overflow.

items.title: Normalize data to match tanh output layers
items.avoid: Tanh output layer when targets are in [0, 255] pixel space.
items.prefer: Scale targets to [−1, 1] before using tanh outputs, or use a different output activation.
items.why: Tanh cannot represent values outside (−1, 1); mismatch wastes capacity and distorts loss.
items.example: Images to [−1,1]: decoder tanh. Labels in {0,1}: sigmoid output, not tanh.
